title: leucanthemum ceratophylloides subsp. tenuifolium
intro: Leucanthemum ceratophylloides subsp. tenuifolium, also known as mountain daisy or alpine daisy, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and slender leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antiviral agent. Leucanthemum ceratophylloides subsp. tenuifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
